text: For every minute you are angry you lose sixty seconds of happiness.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Happiness is when what you think, what you say, and what you do are in harmony.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Count your age by friends, not years. Count your life by smiles, not tears.
author: John Lennon

text: Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.
author: Dalai Lama

text: There is only one happiness in this life, to love and be loved.
author: George Sand

text: The most important thing is to enjoy your life—to be happy—it's all that matters.
author: Audrey Hepburn

text: Happiness is a warm puppy.
author: Charles M. Schulz

text: Folks are usually about as happy as they make their minds up to be.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: The secret of happiness is not in doing what one likes, but in liking what one does.
author: James M. Barrie

text: Happiness resides not in possessions, and not in gold, happiness dwells in the soul.
author: Democritus

text: The greater part of our happiness or misery depends upon our dispositions, and not upon our circumstances.
author: Martha Washington

text: Let us be grateful to the people who make us happy; they are the charming gardeners who make our souls blossom.
author: Marcel Proust

text: It is not how much we have, but how much we enjoy, that makes happiness.
author: Charles Spurgeon

text: Happiness is the natural flower of duty.
author: Phillips Brooks

text: No medicine cures what happiness cannot.
author: Gabriel García Márquez

text: Happiness is holding someone in your arms and knowing you hold the whole world.
author: Orhan Pamuk

text: To be happy, we must not be too concerned with others.
author: Albert Camus

text: A happy life consists not in the absence, but in the mastery of hardships.
author: Helen Keller

text: Happiness is a state of activity.
author: Aristotle

text: Most people would rather be certain they're miserable, than risk being happy.
author: Robert Driskell

text: Happiness depends upon ourselves.
author: Aristotle

text: Enjoy your own life without comparing it with that of another.
author: Marquis de Condorcet

text: The only way to find true happiness is to risk being completely cut open.
author: Chuck Palahniuk

text: Thousands of candles can be lighted from a single candle, and the life of the candle will not be shortened. Happiness never decreases by being shared.
author: Buddha

text: Happiness is an inside job.
author: William Arthur Ward

text: Smile, it is the key that fits the lock of everybody's heart.
author: Anthony J. D'Angelo

text: Joy is a net of love by which you can catch souls.
author: Mother Teresa

text: Optimism is a happiness magnet. If you stay positive, good things and good people will be drawn to you.
author: Mary Lou Retton

text: The best way to cheer yourself is to try to cheer somebody else up.
author: Mark Twain

text: Happiness radiates like the fragrance from a flower and draws all good things towards you.
author: Maharishi Mahesh Yogi

text: Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself, in your way of thinking.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: True happiness involves the full use of one's power and talents.
author: John W. Gardner

text: Happiness is not a goal; it is a by-product.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: Happiness is the interval between periods of unhappiness.
author: Don Marquis

text: Do what makes your soul shine.
author: Anonymous

text: Keep your face to the sunshine and you cannot see a shadow.
author: Helen Keller

text: Now and then it's good to pause in our pursuit of happiness and just be happy.
author: Guillaume Apollinaire

text: If you want to live a happy life, tie it to a goal, not to people or things.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Happiness is peace of mind which comes from self-satisfaction.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: A calm and peaceful mind is the ultimate source of happiness.
author: Dalai Lama
